spec_trace.roadmap_pointer: Retroactive application of HBR-INT-009 (Palmistry/internal_diagnostics diagnostic-wiring mandate, build_rules v1.5.0) to all already-implemented work; three-tier diagnostic model (Flight Recorder kept as business-event ledger + internal_diagnostics internal self-diagnostics + Palmistry external watcher).

draft_scope.intent:
- What: A dedicated retrofit pass whose SOLE focus is to revisit every ALREADY-IMPLEMENTED Work Packet and Micro-Task (the WP-1-* product set + WP-KERNEL-001..012 and their MTs) and ensure each is correctly wired into the three-tier diagnostic model: (1) Flight Recorder (the kept-as-is backend BUSINESS-EVENT ledger - confirm/repair existing FR emits where the behavior is a business event), (2) internal_diagnostics (the NEW Handshake-native INTERNAL self-diagnostics tool - wire panic/health/frame-time/resource + the open diagnostic-event API for in-app runtime behavior), and (3) Palmistry (the NEW EXTERNAL out-of-process watcher - register a tracker where a behavior's freeze/crash/liveness is worth surviving-capture).
- Why: HBR-INT-009 (build_rules v1.5.0, OPERATOR directive 2026-06-27) mandates the diagnostic wiring for every FUTURE build, but the 80+ already-shipped MTs (notably all of WP-KERNEL-012 native editors) predate it and are largely un-instrumented. Without a retrofit, the mandate only governs new work and the existing surface stays a diagnostic blind spot - exactly the gap exposed by the 2026-06-26 live UI-thread freeze the in-process Flight Recorder could not capture.
draft_scope.scope_sketch:
- IN_SCOPE:
  - A systematic inventory of every implemented WP/MT and the runtime behaviors each introduced.
  - For EACH behavior, a recorded diagnostic-wiring verdict against all three tiers: WIRED | NOT_APPLICABLE(reason) | DEFERRED(reason), per HBR-INT-009 evidence_kind=test_run_with_palmistry_consideration_record.
  - Add the missing FR emits / internal_diagnostics instrumentation / Palmistry trackers where the verdict is WIRED.
  - A machine-readable coverage manifest (one row per implemented WP/MT) proving the pass is complete and showing exactly what was wired vs deferred.
  - Priority ordering: highest-risk / most-interactive surfaces first (native editors WP-KERNEL-012, shell WP-KERNEL-011, session/runtime, backend job paths).
- OUT_OF_SCOPE:
  - BUILDING internal_diagnostics or Palmistry themselves (that is the prerequisite build WP-KERNEL-012).
  - Re-opening the Flight Recorder's closed schema (explicitly KEPT AS-IS per operator decision 2026-06-27; new diagnostic event kinds go to internal_diagnostics, not the FR).
  - Any product-behavior change beyond adding diagnostic wiring.
draft_scope.acceptance_criteria:
- Every already-implemented WP and MT has a recorded three-tier diagnostic-wiring verdict (WIRED / NOT_APPLICABLE-with-reason / DEFERRED-with-reason); none silently skipped.
- Every WIRED verdict is backed by a wire test proving the FR emit / internal_diagnostics event / Palmistry tracker actually fires at runtime (not a stub).
- A coverage manifest enumerates all implemented WP/MT with their verdicts; HBR-INT-009 is retroactively satisfiable from the manifest.
- The native-editor surface (WP-KERNEL-012, the most interactive + the freeze origin) is fully instrumented for freeze/crash via Palmistry + internal health via internal_diagnostics.
draft_scope.risks_unknowns:
- HARD DEPENDENCY: cannot start until WP-KERNEL-012 ships internal_diagnostics + Palmistry (you cannot wire into APIs that do not exist). This stub is intentionally parked behind 013.
- Large surface (80+ MTs across 100+ WPs) - needs the same ultracode per-unit pipeline + a strict priority order so it does not become an unbounded sweep; log any coverage caps explicitly (no silent truncation).
- Must not regress shipped behavior - diagnostic wiring is additive-only; each retrofit re-runs the original WP/MT proof to confirm no regression.
- Privacy boundary: internal_diagnostics + Palmistry record NO project/sensitive data; the retrofit must enforce the typed-allowlist schema, never log document/prompt content.
draft_scope.governance_artifact_stance: Repo-governance-light for the mandate trace; product-code (src/) wiring goes through the full WP lifecycle once activated.
draft_scope.scope_split_note: OPERATOR SCOPE DECISION 2026-06-27: this WP's SOLE job is to RECONNECT everything ALREADY BUILT (the 80+ shipped MTs across the WP-1-* + WP-KERNEL-001..012 surface) to the internal internal_diagnostics diagnostics + the external Palmistry watcher. It builds NO new tools and makes NO documentation/spec/governance changes - those were all delivered in WP-KERNEL-012. This WP is additive wiring + a coverage manifest only, and stays parked behind WP-KERNEL-012 (cannot wire into APIs that 015 has not yet shipped).

assumptions_to_attack: A stub is planning metadata, not execution authority.
The retrofit can begin before internal_diagnostics/Palmistry exist (FALSE - hard dependency on WP-KERNEL-013).
Wiring can be claimed WIRED without a runtime wire test (FALSE - must fire at runtime).
Re-opening the FR schema is in scope (FALSE - FR kept as-is; new kinds go to internal_diagnostics).
A full sweep is feasible in one pass without priority ordering or coverage logging.
minimum_controls: Block activation until WP-KERNEL-013 is shipped.
Require a runtime wire test per WIRED verdict.
Require a coverage manifest enumerating ALL implemented WP/MT with explicit verdicts (no silent skip).
Additive-only: re-run each unit's original proof to confirm no regression.
Enforce the no-sensitive-data typed allowlist on every added emit.
